revision.text:
'''Tōdaijiyama Kofun''' (東大寺山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (Kofun) located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. The Kofun is of the {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped mound) type, and its grave goods have been designated as a {{ill|National Treasure of Japan|ja|国宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Treasure of Japan|qq=}}. It is believed to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|qq=}}, and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest {{ill|late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Yayoi period|qq=}} {{ill|highland settlement|ja|高地性集落|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/highland settlement|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|qq=}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|qq=}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|qq=}} from the {{ill|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samida Takarazuka Kofun|qq=}} in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}}. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|qq=}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture's history]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Artifacts in Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[en:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[sn:ōdaijiyama Kofun]]

'''Tōdaijiyama Kofun''' (東大寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofun) located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The Kofun is of the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) type, and its grave goods have been designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]]. It is believed to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]], and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest [[:en:Yayoi period|late Yayoi period]] [[:en:Hill town|highland settlement]] in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|qq=}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|qq=}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|qq=}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|qq=}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Artifacts in Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[en:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[sn:ōdaijiyama Kofun]]

The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|qq=}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|qq=}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|qq=}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Artifacts in Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[en:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[sn:ōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:天理市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]]

The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|qq=}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|qq=}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|qq=}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Artifacts in Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[en:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[sn:ōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:天理市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:東京都の国宝]] [[Category:Inscriptions]]

'''Tōdaijiyama Kofun''' (東大寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofun) located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The Kofun is of the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) type, and its grave goods have been designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]]. It is believed to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]], and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest [[:en:Yayoi period|late Yayoi period]] [[:en:Hill town|highland settlement]] in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|qq=}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|qq=}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|qq=}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|qq=}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Artifacts in Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[sn:ōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:天理市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}}

'''Tōdaijiyama Kofun''' (東大寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofun) located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The Kofun is of the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) type, and its grave goods have been designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]]. It is believed to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]], and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest [[:en:Yayoi period|late Yayoi period]] [[:en:Hill town|highland settlement]] in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|qq=}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|qq=}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|qq=}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|qq=}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Artifacts in Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[sn:ōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara�]] [[Category:天理市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}}

The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|qq=}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|qq=}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[sn:ōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:天理市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}}

revision.text:
'''Tōdaijiyama Kofun''' (東大寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofun) located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The Kofun is of the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) type, and its grave goods have been designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]]. It is believed to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]], and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest [[:en:Yayoi period|late Yayoi period]] [[:en:Hill town|highland settlement]] in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|qq=}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|qq=}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[sn:ōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:天理市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}}

Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|qq=}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|qq=}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[sn:ōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:天理市の Image Requests]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}}

revision.text:
'''Tōdaijiyama Kofun''' (東大寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofun) located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The Kofun is of the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) type, and its grave goods have been designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]]. It is believed to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]], and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest [[:en:Yayoi period|late Yayoi period]] [[:en:Hill town|highland settlement]] in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|qq=}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|qq=}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[sn:ōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:天理市の Image Requests]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}}

Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|qq=}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|qq=}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[sn:ōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}}

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.6214|N|135.837|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3589381]] Wikidata name: Tōdaijiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaijiyama Kofun''' (東大寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofun) located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The Kofun is of the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) type, and its grave goods have been designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]]. It is believed to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]], and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest [[:en:Yayoi period|late Yayoi period]] [[:en:Hill town|highland settlement]] in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|qq=|1=House Mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131456303}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|qq=|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[sn:ōdaijiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}}

The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|qq=|1=House Mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131456303}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. [[sn:commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.6214|N|135.837|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3589381]] Wikidata name: Tōdaijiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaijiyama Kofun''' (東大寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofun) located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The Kofun is of the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) type, and its grave goods have been designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]]. It is believed to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]], and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest [[:en:Yayoi period|late Yayoi period]] [[:en:Hill town|highland settlement]] in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|qq=|1=House Mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131456303}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|qq=|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q3589381}}
