The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|1=House Mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131456303}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q3589381}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.6214|N|135.837|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3589381]] Wikidata name: Tōdaijiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaijiyama Kofun''' (東大寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofun) located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The Kofun is of the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) type, and its grave goods have been designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]]. It is believed to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]], and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest [[:en:Yayoi period|late Yayoi period]] [[:en:Hill town|highland settlement]] in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|1=House Mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131456303}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q3589381}}

Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]], and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest [[:en:Yayoi period|late Yayoi period]] [[:en:Hill town|highland settlement]] in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|1=House Mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131456303}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q3589381}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q3589381}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.6214|N|135.837|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3589381]] Wikidata name: Tōdaijiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaijiyama Kofun''' (東大寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofun) located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The Kofun is of the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) type, and its grave goods have been designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]]. It is believed to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]], and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest [[:en:Yayoi period|late Yayoi period]] [[:en:Hill town|highland settlement]] in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|1=House Mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131456303}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - This region was the base of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]], and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest [[:en:Yayoi period|late Yayoi period]] [[:en:Hill town|highland settlement]] in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|1=House Mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131456303}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q3589381}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q3589381}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q3589381}}

[[commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] [[commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.6214|N|135.837|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3589381]] Wikidata name: Tōdaijiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaijiyama Kofun''' (東大寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofun) located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The Kofun is of the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) type, and its grave goods have been designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]]. It is believed to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]], and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest [[:en:Yayoi period|late Yayoi period]] [[:en:Hill town|highland settlement]] in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|1=House Mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131456303}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q3589381}}

It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q3589381}} [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] [[co:commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.6214|N|135.837|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3589381]] Wikidata name: Tōdaijiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaijiyama Kofun''' (東大寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofun) located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The Kofun is of the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) type, and its grave goods have been designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]]. It is believed to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]], and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest [[:en:Yayoi period|late Yayoi period]] [[:en:Hill town|highland settlement]] in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|1=House Mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131456303}} from the [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samida Takarazuka Kofun]] in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q3589381}} [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] [[co:commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.6214|N|135.837|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3589381]] Wikidata name: Tōdaijiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaijiyama Kofun''' (東大寺山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (Kofun) located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture. The Kofun is of the {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mound) type, and its grave goods have been designated as a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure of Japan}}. It is believed to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}}, and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=late Yayoi period}} {{ill|Hill town|en|Hill town|WD=Q5762461|lt=highland settlement}} in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|1=House Mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131456303}} from the {{ill|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|en|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079|lt=Samida Takarazuka Kofun}} in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q3589381}} [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|1=House Mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131456303}} from the {{ill|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|en|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079|lt=Samida Takarazuka Kofun}} in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q3589381}} [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] [[co:commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tōdaijiyama Kofun]][[ja:東大寺山古墳]][[ko:토다이지야마 고분]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.6214|N|135.837|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Tōdaijiyama Kofun''' (東大寺山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (Kofun) located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara|1=Tenri, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture. The Kofun is of the {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mound) type, and its grave goods have been designated as a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure of Japan|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}. It is believed to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Among its grave goods is a sword with an engraved inscription featuring 24 characters in gold inlay, including a date referencing the "Zhongping" (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty, corresponding to the years 184–189 CE. == Overview == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is situated in a hilly area spanning Wani to Ichinomoto in Tenri City. This region was the base of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan|1=Wani clan}}, and many related ancient Kofuns are found along the ridges, about 70 meters above the surrounding plain. The kofun is one of several attributed to this influential clan. On the same ridge lies the largest {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=late Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} {{ill|Hill town|en|Hill town|WD=Q5762461|lt=highland settlement|1=Hill town}} in Nara Prefecture. The settlement covers an area approximately 400 meters east to west and 300 meters north to south. It featured pit dwellings surrounded by a double moat, with a structure similar to that of the {{ill|Kannonyama Site|ja|観音寺山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonyama Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izumi, Osaka Prefecture. The Tōdaijiyama Kofun overlaps this earlier settlement, and it is estimated to have been constructed 150–200 years after the settlement ceased to function. == Dimensions, Shape, and Burial Facilities == The Tōdaijiyama Kofun is a zenpō-kōen-fun, approximately 140 meters in length. The mound is surrounded by a ring of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}. Its construction is estimated to date to the late 4th century. The burial facility within the posterior circular portion consists of a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} covering a wooden coffin aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. The burial chamber, measuring an estimated 9.4 meters in length, was a large-scale structure but had been looted. == Grave Goods == The burial chamber contained two burial pits to the east and west, which housed an abundance of weapons and other grave goods. Among these, a sword with an inscription referencing the Zhongping (中平) era of the Later Han Dynasty was discovered in the eastern burial pit. The grave goods include 20 iron swords, 9 iron daggers, various types of jade, stone armlets, and steatite jars. Five swords from the burial site feature decorative pommels, including a "Zhongping" inscribed sword. The sword guards exhibit various designs, predominantly trefoil-shaped rings similar to those found at {{ill|Sekiganri Kofun|ja|石巌里古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiganri Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Lelang. Such swords were also excavated from the {{ill|Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja|若八幡宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Hachiman Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Fukuoka. One of the swords features a pommel shaped like a house, resembling depictions of buildings on mirrors, such as the {{ill|House-patterned Mirror|ja|家屋文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-patterned Mirror|1=House Mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131456303}} from the {{ill|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|en|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079|lt=Samida Takarazuka Kofun|1=Samida Takarazuka Kofun}} in Kawai, Nara Prefecture. === The "Zhongping" Inscribed Sword === A 110-centimeter-long iron sword featuring a 24-character gold-inlay inscription was excavated. The inscription reads:
"Zhongping □□ (year), fifth month, day of Bingwu: This sword, forged of refined steel, was created and inscribed. It is a blade of great purity and strength, aligned with the stars above and averting misfortune below."
The inscription references the Zhongping era of Emperor Ling of Han, corresponding to 184–189 CE. This period marks the end of the "Wa Kingdom's Turmoil" (倭国乱) mentioned in Chinese records. The sword is believed to have been a gift from the Later Han dynasty following the end of the turmoil. While its exact provenance and how it came to be buried in the Tōdaijiyama Kofun remain uncertain, the sword suggests a link between the local elites and the Later Han dynasty. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === The grave goods from the Tōdaijiyama Kofun were collectively designated as a National Treasure of Japan in 2017. They are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. The designation includes the following items: - 1 gold-inlaid flower-decorated ring-pommel sword - 2 flower-decorated ring-pommel swords - 2 house-shaped ring-pommel swords - 249 metal objects - 102 stone objects - 59 jade pieces - 2 organic material fragments - (Accompanying designation) 9 fragments of bronze arrowheads - (Accompanying designation) Miscellaneous stone fragments - (Accompanying designation) 4 fragments of haniwa == References == == Further Reading == - Supervising Organization: {{ill|Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|ja|文化財研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Independent Administrative Institution for Cultural Properties|1=National Research Institutes for Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499886}}, Nara Division, "Japanese Archaeology: The 'Dawn of Civilization' Exhibition," Shogakukan, 2005, ISBN 4-09-681821-6. == External Links == - [http://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_free_page/index.php?id=1394 Tokyo National Museum: Special Exhibition on Tōdaijiyama Kofun Swords (2011)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺山古墳|version=104552175|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:東大寺山古墳]] {{Commons category|Tōdaijiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q3589381}} [[Category:Collection of the Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Image requests in Tenri City]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]
